title: Microcharis remotiflora
taxonomy: Microcharis remotiflora is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and the genus Microcharis. Microcharis remotiflora is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the countries of Kenya and Tanzania. The plant is classified as a perennial herb that produces small, white or purple flowers. Its classification further categorizes it under the family Fabaceae, subfamily Faboideae, and genus Microcharis.

intro: Microcharis remotiflora is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Microcharis remotiflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
